fix: increase kiosk MAX_WAIT 120->600s for first boot docker build time

N2840 first boot: 4 Docker images must be built (~30-60 min).
120s timeout was too short - browser opened to error page and got stuck.
600s gives enough margin for first boot without affecting normal boots
(containers start in <30s normally, kiosk exits wait loop early).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: guide grab handles + single-pixel snap

Each guide now has two frames:
- Guide line: full-screen strip (unchanged, still full hit area)
- Handle badge: pill-shaped frame centred on screen, sits on the guide line.
  Shows pixel position + drag-direction arrow (↕ or ↔).
  Brightens on hover; both badge and line highlight together.

Snap behaviour:
- Dragging now snaps to nearest individual physical pixel (floor(rawPx+0.5))
  instead of N-pixel boundaries — gives full single-pixel precision
- Initial placement (Add H/V Guide button) also uses single-pixel snap
- Removed SnapPx() helper (was only used by guide code)

Cleanup:
- RemoveGuideFrame() now correctly tears down handle badge frame
- Removed redundant _currentPx / _snapN state from guide frames
- Removed GUIDE_LOCKED_COLOR and snap-indicator logic (obsolete)
- Handle delegates drag state to guide frame (single OnUpdate per guide)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Multi-target net8.0 and net9.0 for broader runtime compatibility (#93)

* Multi-target net8.0 and net9.0 for broader runtime compatibility

- Change TargetFramework to TargetFrameworks net8.0;net9.0 in AlRunner.csproj
  and AlRunner.Tests.csproj
- Use conditional package references for System.Diagnostics.EventLog and
  System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager (8.0.x for net8.0, 9.0.x for net9.0)
- Update CI workflows to install both .NET 8 and .NET 9 SDKs via setup-dotnet
  array syntax and run tests for each framework separately
- NuGet package will now include tools/net8.0/ and tools/net9.0/ so dotnet tool
  install automatically selects the matching runtime

Closes #75

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CopyBCDlls target for multi-targeting

When switching from TargetFramework (single) to TargetFrameworks (multi),
MSBuild introduces an outer build where $(TargetFramework) is empty. The
CopyBCDlls target was evaluating _AlRunnerOut with an empty TFM segment,
producing paths like 'AlRunner/bin/Debug//dll' that don't exist.

Fix: add Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' != ''" so CopyBCDlls only runs
in inner builds (where $(TargetFramework) is set to net8.0 or net9.0).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix parallel download conflict and ServerTests framework ambiguity

Two issues introduced by multi-targeting:

1. EnsureALCompiler and EnsureBCServiceTierDlls targets in AlRunner.csproj
   now run for each TFM inner build. MSBuild runs inner builds in parallel,
   so net8.0 and net9.0 builds both try to download the same DLLs to the
   same shared cache path simultaneously, causing IOException (file in use).
   Fix: add 'TargetFramework == net8.0' condition so downloads only run
   once, in the net8.0 inner build. The net9.0 inner build finds the
   files already present.

2. CliServer.StartAsync() calls 'dotnet run --no-build --project AlRunner'
   which is ambiguous on a multi-targeted project — dotnet run requires
   --framework when TargetFrameworks (plural) is set. The server exits
   immediately, causing 'Server process exited before signaling readiness'.
   Fix: derive the current TFM from Environment.Version and pass
   --framework to dotnet run, matching the framework under which the
   test is running.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix download targets to run in outer build for multi-targeting

With <TargetFrameworks> (plural), MSBuild dispatches inner builds in
parallel. The previous fix (net8.0-only condition) caused net9.0's
ResolveAssemblyReferences to run before net8.0's download completed,
resulting in 'assembly not found' errors.

Correct fix: run EnsureALCompiler and EnsureBCServiceTierDlls in the
OUTER build via BeforeTargets=DispatchToInnerBuilds. This ensures DLLs
are fully present before either inner build starts.

Condition: 'TargetFrameworks == empty (single-target) OR TargetFramework
== empty (outer build)'. In inner builds, both TargetFrameworks and
TargetFramework are set, so the condition is false and no download is
attempted. In single-target builds, TargetFrameworks is empty so the
target runs before ResolveAssemblyReferences as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CliRunner to pass --framework for multi-targeted project

dotnet run on a multi-targeted project requires --framework or it fails
immediately with exit code 1 and no output. IntegrationTests use CliRunner
which was missing --framework, causing all integration tests to fail.

Derive the TFM from Environment.Version (same as CliServer fix) to always
match the framework under which the test process is running.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Document Render auto-detection solution for chicken-and-egg deployment problem

Updated RENDER_QUICK_START.md:
- Simplified backend ALLOWED_ORIGINS to use wildcard: https://*.onrender.com
- Removed need for VITE_API_URL on frontend (auto-detects)
- Eliminated chicken-and-egg problem in deployment

Added RENDER_AUTO_DETECTION.md:
- Explains how auto-detection works
- Compares three deployment strategies
- Shows why wildcard ALLOWED_ORIGINS is safe in this context
- Provides verification steps

The frontend's apiClient.ts automatically detects backend URL by:
- Taking frontend hostname: med-app-frontend-xxx.onrender.com
- Replacing 'frontend' with 'backend'
- Result: med-app-backend-xxx.onrender.com

This works at runtime, solving the URL ordering problem elegantly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(starrocks): increase FE/CN resource allocations to resolve FE Full GC

The Superset/Iceberg metadata query planner was timing out (~8s) due to
FE Full GC triggered by insufficient heap headroom.  Two root causes:

1. _FE_CONFIG_BASE hardcoded -Xmx8192m regardless of the container
   memory limit, leaving the JVM heap at 8 GiB even in environments
   where the container had 16 GiB available (no room for off-heap
   allocations: metaspace, code cache, direct buffers, GC overhead).

2. QA stack had severely under-resourced FE and CN pods (1 CPU / 8 Gi)
   relative to StarRocks' minimum recommendation (8 CPU / 16 Gi for FE;
   16 CPU / 64 Gi for BE/CN).

Changes:
- JVM heap is now derived as 87.5% of memory_limit (leaves ~12.5% for
  off-heap) and is configurable via starrocks:fe_config:jvm_heap_mb.
- Code defaults updated to match StarRocks production recommendations:
  FE 8 CPU / 16 Gi; BE/CN 16 CPU / 32 Gi.
- QA stack: FE 1000m→8000m CPU, 8Gi→16Gi RAM + jvm_heap_mb 14336;
  CN 1000m→16000m CPU, 8Gi→32Gi RAM.
- Production stack: FE 2000m→8000m CPU + jvm_heap_mb 14336 (memory
  already 16Gi); CN 2000m→16000m CPU, 16Gi→32Gi RAM.

Ref: https://docs.starrocks.io/docs/deployment/plan_cluster/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ux-8): Split violations into [CONFIG] vs [ARCH] categories

CONFIG: Quick 10-minute fixes (add frontmatter, update descriptions, etc)
ARCH: Structural improvements requiring migration (real token savings)

Example output:

  [CONFIG] Quick fixes — 10 minutes each:
    skills/pdf/SKILL.md | Description is 6 chars

  [ARCH] Structural improvements (real token savings):
    skills/pdf/SKILL.md | Ungated skill eligible for migration

This prevents alert fatigue by helping users distinguish between
trivial fixes and impactful architectural changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Keep androidAutoConnected flag on session disconnect

The periodic AA session disconnects were clearing androidAutoConnected,
which allowed the focus-retry logic to fight Google Assistant and other
apps requesting transient audio focus (e.g. voice messages, nav prompts).

Now androidAutoConnected is only cleared by AUDIO_BECOMING_NOISY (when
BT truly disconnects), ensuring focus-retry is suppressed the entire
time the user is in the car.

commit.message:
fix: resolve all open Dependabot security alerts + add changes.md

Pin vulnerable transitive dependencies in pyproject.toml to close
all 5 open Dependabot alerts (alerts #110–#119):

- cryptography >= 46.0.7  (buffer overflow, medium; DNS constraint, low)
- Pygments >= 2.20.0       (ReDoS, low)
- requests >= 2.33.0       (insecure temp file reuse, medium)
- poetry >= 2.3.3          (wheel path traversal, high)

uv.lock and requirements.txt regenerated accordingly.

Also adds changes.md summarising all security and notebook fixes
made in this maintenance pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add --output-junit flag for JUnit XML CI test reporting (#94)

Add a new --output-junit <path> CLI flag that writes a standard JUnit XML
test report alongside normal console output. This enables native test result
display in GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps, and GitLab CI pipelines.

Changes:
- AlRunner/JUnitReport.cs: new static class with WriteJUnit() method.
  Groups tests by codeunit name as <testsuite> elements.
  Uses <failure> for assertion failures, <error> for runner limitations.
  Time values are in seconds (invariant decimal separator).
- AlRunner/Pipeline.cs: add OutputJunitPath to PipelineOptions; add
  CodeunitName to TestResult (populated from SourceFileMapper); call
  JUnitReport.WriteJUnit() when OutputJunitPath is set.
- AlRunner/Program.cs: parse --output-junit <path> CLI flag; update
  --help text; update PrintGuide() with JUnit XML section.
- AlRunner.Tests/JUnitOutputTests.cs: 9 xUnit tests covering unit and
  integration behaviour.
- CHANGELOG.md: document the new feature.

Closes #72

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Deduplicate repeated error blocks in PrintResults output (#70) (#95)

When many tests are blocked by the same runner error, PrintResults
previously repeated the identical error message block once per test,
generating hundreds of lines of noise in the output.

Changes:
- Add bool verbose = false parameter to Executor.PrintResults
- Group Error tests by message; if a message appears >= 2 times, print
  it once as a WARN block with a count, then print compact
  'ERROR TestName (blocked)' lines for each affected test
- With -v / verbose=true, fall back to full per-test detail (no dedup)
- Single/unique errors still show full detail (no WARN, no dedup)
- FAIL tests are never deduplicated
- Pipeline.cs passes options.Verbose to PrintResults

Before (66 blocked tests, same error):
  ERROR Test1
        CompilationExcludedException: Codeunit 50123 was excluded...
        Inject this dependency via an AL interface.
  ERROR Test2
        CompilationExcludedException: Codeunit 50123 was excluded...
  ... (66 × 3 lines = 198 lines of noise)

After:
  WARN  CompilationExcludedException: Codeunit 50123 was excluded...
        (66 tests blocked — use -v for per-test details)

  ERROR Test1 (blocked)
  ERROR Test2 (blocked)
  ... (3 + 66 lines total)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: optimize CLI params, trim service hardening, update docs

CLI short options normalized to lowercase mnemonics:
  -S → -m (--shutdown-mode → --mode)
  -D → -d (--delay)
  -L → -l (--log-level)
  -M → -s (--systemd)

Environment variables simplified:
  LINKSTAY_SHUTDOWN_MODE → LINKSTAY_MODE
  LINKSTAY_DELAY_MINUTES → LINKSTAY_DELAY

Service file trimmed to essential hardening only:
  Kept: NoNewPrivileges, CapabilityBoundingSet, ProtectSystem=strict,
        PrivateTmp, ProtectHome, RestrictAddressFamilies, SystemCallFilter,
        resource limits (MemoryMax, TasksMax, OOMScoreAdjust).
  Removed ~20 redundant defense-in-depth directives that protected
  against threats irrelevant to a simple ICMP monitor daemon.

Updated: README.md parameter table, copilot-instructions.md, test.sh.
All 35/35 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix deprecated Keras/TF API calls in deep learning notebooks

lectures/05a_deep_learning.ipynb:
- Cell 25: replace deprecated 'from tensorflow.keras import layers, Model'
  with 'from keras import layers, Model' (Keras 3.x standalone API)

lectures/05b_deep_learning.ipynb:
- Cell 1: remove 'import tensorflow as tf' (TF not in requirements)
- Cell 3 (sampling func): replace tf.random.normal/tf.shape/tf.keras.backend.exp
  with keras.random.normal/keras.ops.shape/keras.ops.exp
- Cells 5 & 7 (CustomVariationalLayer): replace all tf.keras.backend.*
  with keras.ops.* equivalents:
    tf.keras.backend.flatten(x) -> keras.ops.reshape(x, (-1,))
    tf.keras.backend.mean(...)  -> keras.ops.mean(...)
    tf.keras.backend.square(x) -> keras.ops.square(x)
    tf.keras.backend.exp(x)    -> keras.ops.exp(x)

All changes target Keras 3.x (keras==3.13.2) which removed the legacy
keras.backend functional API in favor of keras.ops (numpy-style ops).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(e2e): pin health check to HTTP endpoint to avoid dev cert errors in CI

WithHttpHealthCheck without endpointName defaults to the HTTPS endpoint,
causing UntrustedRoot SSL errors on the Linux CI runner where the ASP.NET
dev certificate is not trusted. Explicitly target the 'http' endpoint.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add file logging and decouple model loading from app startup

- Add rotating file logging to ~/.talkrefine/talkrefine.log (5MB, 3 backups)
- Dual handler: file (timestamped) + stdout (plain)
- Replace all print() calls with logger.info/exception
- Log ASR/LLM load times and transcription timings
- Add _models_ready flag; show 'Model still loading...' if F6 pressed early
- Register hotkeys and start UI before model loading
- Load models in background thread with error handling
- On model failure: log error, show in overlay, keep app running

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: Living Digital Twin DR Platform

Full-stack MVP for Disaster Recovery simulation:
- Neo4j graph DB for infrastructure topology
- VictoriaMetrics for live DR metrics (RTO/RPO)
- Qdrant vector DB for documentation RAG
- FastAPI backend with MCP server (3 DR tools)
- React + ForceGraph3D dashboard with disaster simulation
- Cross-platform launch scripts (PowerShell + WSL2)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: ai-digital-twin-dr
repository.description: Living Digital Twin for Disaster Recovery: Neo4j graph + VictoriaMetrics + Qdrant RAG + FastAPI MCP Server + React 3D dashboard
